name: David Foster Wallace
question: When was David Foster Wallace born?
gold_answer: David Foster Wallace was born on February 21, 1962.

name: David Foster Wallace
question: What is David Foster Wallace renowned for?
gold_answer: He is renowned as an American novelist, short story writer, essayist, and university professor of English and creative writing.

name: David Foster Wallace
question: Which David Foster Wallace novel is considered one of the 100 best English-language novels from 1923 to 2005 by Time magazine?
gold_answer: His novel "Infinite Jest," published in 1996, was cited as one of the best.

name: David Foster Wallace
question: What is the title of David Foster Wallace's posthumous novel that was a finalist for the Pulitzer Prize for Fiction in 2012?
gold_answer: The novel is titled "The Pale King."

name: David Foster Wallace
question: How did the Los Angeles Times's David Ulin describe David Foster Wallace?
gold_answer: David Ulin called him "one of the most influential and innovative writers of the last twenty years."

name: David Foster Wallace
question: Where did David Foster Wallace grow up?
gold_answer: He grew up in Illinois.

name: David Foster Wallace
question: Which college did David Foster Wallace attend?
gold_answer: He attended Amherst College.

name: David Foster Wallace
question: At which colleges did David Foster Wallace teach English?
gold_answer: He taught at Emerson College, Illinois State University, and Pomona College.

name: David Foster Wallace
question: What year did David Foster Wallace pass away?
gold_answer: He passed away in 2008.

name: David Foster Wallace
question: How old was David Foster Wallace at the time of his death?
gold_answer: He was 46 years old.

question: What titles did Lord Byron hold?

question: What are two of Lord Byron's most famous works?
gold_answer: Two of his most famous works are "Don Juan" and "Childe Harold's Pilgrimage."

question: What literary movement is Lord Byron associated with?

name: Lord Byron
question: How long did Lord Byron live in Italy?
gold_answer: Lord Byron lived in Italy for seven years.

gold_answer: He lived in Venice, Ravenna, and Pisa.

gold_answer: He was forced to flee England due to threats of lynching.

name: Friedrich Nietzsche
question: When was Friedrich Nietzsche born?
gold_answer: Friedrich Nietzsche was born on October 15, 1844.

name: Friedrich Nietzsche
question: What was Friedrich Nietzsche's nationality?
gold_answer: Friedrich Nietzsche was German.

name: Friedrich Nietzsche
question: At what age did Friedrich Nietzsche become the Chair of Classical Philology at the University of Basel?
gold_answer: He became the Chair at the age of 24.

name: Friedrich Nietzsche
question: Why did Friedrich Nietzsche resign from his position at the University of Basel?
gold_answer: Nietzsche resigned in 1879 due to health problems that plagued him most of his life.

name: Friedrich Nietzsche
question: What major health crisis did Friedrich Nietzsche experience in 1889?
gold_answer: In 1889, Nietzsche suffered a collapse and afterward a complete loss of his mental faculties, leading to paralysis and probably vascular dementia.

name: Friedrich Nietzsche
question: How did Friedrich Nietzsche spend his final years?
gold_answer: He lived under the care of his mother until her death in 1897 and then with his sister Elisabeth Förster-Nietzsche until his own death in 1900.

name: Friedrich Nietzsche
question: When did Friedrich Nietzsche die?
gold_answer: Friedrich Nietzsche died on August 25, 1900.

name: Friedrich Nietzsche
question: What are some prominent themes in Friedrich Nietzsche's philosophical work?
gold_answer: His themes include the critique of truth and morality, the notion of the "will to power," the theory of the Übermensch, and the concept of eternal return.

name: Friedrich Nietzsche
question: What is the concept of "Übermensch" in Friedrich Nietzsche's philosophy?
gold_answer: The Übermensch is a concept in Nietzsche's philosophy representing an individual who has overcome established values to create new ones, embodying the evolution beyond human morality.

name: Friedrich Nietzsche
question: What does "will to power" signify in Nietzsche's thought?
gold_answer: The "will to power" describes a fundamental drive present in individuals to assert and enhance their power and influence.
